Class ProofEnvironment

java.lang.Object
de.uka.ilkd.key.proof.mgt.ProofEnvironment
public class ProofEnvironment extends Object
The unique environment a proof is performed in. The environment consists of a java model, specifications, and a set of justified rules. Since the starting point of the proofs contained in the environment is equal, there is an InitConfig contained to be used to start proofs of this environment.

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    ProofEnvironment(InitConfig initConfig)
    constructs a proof environment with the given initial configuration of the proofs contained in the environment.

  • Constructor Details

    • ProofEnvironment

      public ProofEnvironment(InitConfig initConfig)
      constructs a proof environment with the given initial configuration of the proofs contained in the environment.

  • Method Details

    • getJavaModel

      public JavaModel getJavaModel()
      retrieves the java model underlying this environment.

    • getServicesForEnvironment

      public Services getServicesForEnvironment()
      returns the Services instance for the environment
      Returns:
      the Services instance for the environment

    • getInitConfigForEnvironment

      public InitConfig getInitConfigForEnvironment()
      returns the initial configuration of which a copy can be used to load proofs belonging to this environment.

    • registerProof

      public void registerProof(ProofOblInput po, ProofAggregate pl)
      registers a proof loaded with the given ProofOblInput. The method adds the proof list generated by the input to the suitable contract if one is found. In all cases the proof is added to the proofs of this environment and the proofs are marked to belong to this environment.

    • addProofEnvironmentListener

      public void addProofEnvironmentListener(ProofEnvironmentListener l)

      public void removeProofEnvironmentListener(ProofEnvironmentListener l)

      public boolean equals(Object cmp)
      returns true iff the java model equals those of the argument proof environment. TODO: extend to available rules and specs.
